title: Introduction to Image Processing and Computer Vision

prerequisites.text: Admission into the Master's degree specialising in Computer Science or Artificial Intelligence, or permission from the course convener.

outline: To introduce students to basic concepts in computer vision and image processing, oriented towards solving real world, practical image analysis problems. Topics include: Basic Signal processing; Image Transforms and Operations; Feature Detection; Object Descriptions; Basic Segmentation and Registration; Fundamental Segmentation techniques; Machine Learning and GAs in Computer Vision. Case studies and topical papers are discussed.
assessment: Exam: Open Book, 2 hours. Class Record: Practical 60%, Review Questions 40%. Final Mark: Exam 40%, Class Record 60%.
